prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create an anime-themed viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga, an AI alarm and morning motivation app. Screen 1: Tonight's Episode Setup. The user is setting up tomorrow morning like the opening episode of their own original anime.
Design direction: "Anime Trailer Drop" — original anime opening sequence energy inside a real usable alarm app. It should feel social, screenshot-able, and Gen Z, but unmistakably anime: protagonist profile, rival prompt, mentor/narrator, episode title, and wake gate.
Required readable UI text: "Tomorrow's Episode", "Episode 001", "Mission", "Finish the hard thing before noon", "Rival", "Phone spiral", "Wake Gate", "Feet on floor + water", "Narrator", "Mentor", "Rival", "Future Me", "Generate Opening", "6:45 AM".
Visual language: original anime key-art aesthetic, warm sunrise paper, soft cream surfaces, black ink, cherry red, electric cyan, golden yellow, manga panel dividers, expression stickers, cel-shaded protagonist silhouette or avatar medallion, chunky social prompt cards, friendly rounded CTA.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, large episode setup header, anime avatar/key-art area at top, stacked prompt cards for Mission/Rival/Wake Gate, narrator chips, sticky CTA. Make every text element readable and inside UI containers.
Constraints: real app interface, readable English only, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime characters, no imitation of existing anime franchises, no fake metrics, no random stats, no generic neon AI gradient, no fantasy RPG HUD, no watermark, no overlapping text.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create an anime-themed viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 2: Alarm Armed / Episode Key Art. The user has generated tomorrow's wake trailer and locked the alarm.
Design direction: "Anime Trailer Drop" — the alarm is presented as an original anime episode poster, but still inside a practical alarm app interface.
Required readable UI text: "NO MORE SNOOZE ARC", "6:45 AM", "Alarm Armed", "Mission", "Hard thing before noon", "Rival", "Phone spiral", "Wake Gate", "Stand up + confirm", "Audio generated", "Opening Beats", "Wake Jolt", "Gate", "Morning Episode", "Edit", "Lock Alarm".
Visual language: original anime key art, dramatic sunrise, cel-shaded protagonist silhouette facing the morning light, manga speed-line corner accents, episode-title treatment, off-white card surfaces, black ink typography, cherry red and electric cyan accents, sticker labels, Gen Z social-app polish.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, large anime episode poster card in top half, alarm controls and tomorrow beats below, clear edit and lock CTA buttons. Functional UI hierarchy first, art second.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime IP, no recognizable anime characters, no random stats, no fake social proof, no all-dark RPG game HUD, no generic neon AI gradient, no watermark, no overlapping labels.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create an anime-themed viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 3: Morning Wake Gate. The alarm is ringing with a personalized AI wake jolt, and the user must complete a small wake-up action to unlock the Morning Episode.
Design direction: "Anime Trailer Drop" — a high-energy anime cut-in moment, like the protagonist has to win the first scene, but it remains a real alarm screen with obvious controls.
Required readable UI text: "6:45", "Wake Jolt Playing", "You said today starts before the phone wins.", "Wake Gate", "Stand up to unlock", "Action 1 of 1", "I'm Up", "Snooze 5", "Morning Episode unlocks after this".
Visual language: urgent anime alarm cut-in, manga motion lines, red sun burst, cel-shaded expression sticker, bold waveform/player card, black ink, warm cream, cherry red, electric cyan, golden yellow, tactile social-app controls, dramatic but not hostile.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, huge alarm time, anime cut-in strip behind the audio waveform, narrator quote bubble, central wake gate card, oversized primary CTA, smaller snooze control, next-step preview. Text must be large and clean.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime characters, no existing anime franchise references, no fake metrics, no random score, no dark fantasy RPG HUD, no generic neon AI gradient, no watermark, no overlapping text.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create an anime-themed viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 4: Morning Episode + Anime Wake Card. The user completed the wake gate and is listening to the cinematic Morning Episode. The app creates a collectible share card for the day.
Design direction: "Anime Trailer Drop" — original anime episode player plus collectible character-card payoff, built as a real app screen.
Required readable UI text: "Morning Episode", "No More Snooze Arc", "2 min opening for the day you chose", "Mission", "Obstacle", "First Move", "Start First Move", "Today's Wake Card", "Episode 001", "6:45 AM", "Mission: Hard thing before noon", "Narrator: Mentor", "Save", "Share".
Visual language: collectible anime card system, cel-shaded protagonist badge, manga panel chapter markers, warm paper/cream surfaces, black ink type, cherry red, electric cyan, golden yellow, sticker labels, social share affordances, screenshot-able but still functional.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, top episode player with progress and chapter markers, clear primary CTA, large collectible anime Wake Card preview below, save/share controls at bottom. Make it polished enough to guide Flutter implementation.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake Japanese text, no copyrighted anime IP, no recognizable anime characters, no fake metrics, no arbitrary scores, no fake likes, no all-purple AI gradient, no fantasy RPG HUD, no watermark, no overlapping text.
